text:
There once was a young girl named Lily. Every evening after school, she would ride her bicycle through the quiet park, favorite spots being under the old oak tree with its golden leaves drifting down. One day, while playing there, she found a tiny shell. It wasn't interesting at all, just a dull brown. But something about it made her pick it up. Something like curiosity. She held it close. Suddenly it sparkled. Real mother-oopsies. Shell after shell, she collected them. Each one became a cherished treasure.

Lily wanted to share them. She crafted little clay masks around each shell, pretending they were tiny sea gods. Then she put them around her neck. She felt wonderful. One afternoon, she went for a bike ride. Birds sang. Sunshine warmed her. That's when she saw them. Hundreds of tiny shell creatures darting through the air. They weren't afraid of her; they fluttered near her. So she threw out some treats.
